summary: chain-key-validator exfiltrates host and environment data to attacker-controlled GitHub Pages C2
details: The package executes a system-information harvester both from a package.json lifecycle script and from scanner.js. The code requires os, https, fs, and child_process, collects host identifiers (os.hostname(), process.cwd(), output of execSync('whoami') and similar shell commands) and process.env, and POSTs the result to a hardcoded endpoint at https://ddjidd564.github.io. scanner.js additionally performs filesystem probing (fs.existsSync on multiple paths) and uses curl/https.request to relay collected data to the same GitHub Pages-hosted C2. The destination is an unrelated personal GitHub Pages site, not the package's publisher infrastructure, and the package's stated purpose (a 'chain key validator') has no need to read environment variables, hostname, or arbitrary filesystem contents and ship them off-host. Installing or loading this package leaks installer host identity, working directory, environment variables, and system metadata to the attacker.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
